2603.17204.title: CODMAS: A Dialectic Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework for Structured RTL Optimization
2603.17204.paper_summary: Optimizing Register Transfer Level (RTL) code is a critical step in Electronic Design Automation (EDA) for improving power, performance, and area (PPA). We present CODMAS (Collaborative Optimization via a Dialectic Multi-Agent System), a framework that combines structured dialectic reasoning with domain-aware code generation and deterministic evaluation to automate RTL optimization. At the core of CODMAS are two dialectic agents: the Articulator, inspired by rubber-duck debugging, which articulates stepwise transformation plans and exposes latent assumptions; and the Hypothesis Partner, which predicts outcomes and reconciles deviations between expected and actual behavior to guide targeted refinements. These agents direct a Domain-Specific Coding Agent (DCA) to generate architecture-aware Verilog edits and a Code Evaluation Agent (CEA) to verify syntax, functionality, and PPA metrics. We introduce RTLOPT, a benchmark of 120 Verilog triples (unoptimized, optimized, testbench) for pipelining and clock-gating transformations. Across proprietary and open LLMs, CODMAS achieves ~25% reduction in critical path delay for pipelining and ~22% power reduction for clock gating, while reducing functional and compilation failures compared to strong prompting and agentic baselines. These results demonstrate that structured multi-agent reasoning can significantly enhance automated RTL optimization and scale to more complex designs and broader optimization tasks.
2603.17204.paper_summary_zh: 優化寄存器傳輸級 (RTL) 代碼是電子設計自動化 (EDA) 中改善功耗、性能和面積 (PPA) 的關鍵步驟。<lb/>我們提出了 CODMAS（通過辯證多代理系統進行協作優化），這是一個將結構化辯證推理與領域感知代碼生成和確定性評估相結合的框架，以自動化 RTL 優化。<lb/>CODMAS 的核心是兩個辯證代理：啟發於橡膠鴨調試的表達者，該代理闡明逐步轉換計劃並揭示潛在假設；以及假設夥伴，該代理預測結果並調和預期行為與實際行為之間的偏差，以指導針對性的改進。<lb/>這些代理指導一個特定領域的編碼代理 (DCA) 生成架構感知的 Verilog 編輯，以及一個代碼評估代理 (CEA) 來驗證語法、功能和 PPA 指標。<lb/>我們介紹 RTLOPT，一個包含 120 個 Verilog 三元組（未優化、優化、測試平台）的基準，用於流水線和時鐘閘變換。<lb/>在專有和開放的 LLM 中，CODMAS 在流水線方面實現了約 25% 的關鍵路徑延遲減少，並在時鐘閘方面實現了約 22% 的功耗減少，同時減少了與強提示和代理基準相比的功能和編譯失敗。<lb/>這些結果表明，結構化的多代理推理可以顯著增強自動化 RTL 優化，並擴展到更複雜的設計和更廣泛的優化任務。
2603.17204.author: Che-Ming Chang et.al.
2603.17204.authors: Che-Ming Chang, Prashanth Vijayaraghavan, Ashutosh Jadhav, Charles Mackin, Vandana Mukherjee, Hsinyu Tsai, Ehsan Degan
